title: eriope crassipes subsp. crassipes
intro: Eriope crassipes subsp. crassipes, also known as the 'Kanuka', is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its small, needle-like leaves and fragrant white flowers. The leaves of this plant contain essential oils with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for various medicinal purposes. The medicinal properties of Kanuka oil have been found to have health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Eriope crassipes subsp. crassipes is often used in herbal preparations like salves, creams, and teas to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
